title: broussonetia zanthoxylon
intro: Broussonetia zanthoxylon, also known as the 'Rhatany' or 'Ratanhia', is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Moraceae. The plant's roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat oral infections, wounds, and other health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Broussonetia zanthoxylon have been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting against bacterial infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Broussonetia zanthoxylon include infusions and tinctures made from the root, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
